subject: Computer Networking
title: ISPs and How You Connect
description: A fast breakdown of ISPs and How You Connect for builders.

hook.text: You pay one company for internet. That company connects you to the rest of the world. That's your ISP.

buildup.text: Tier 3 ISPs serve homes and businesses. Tier 2 ISPs serve regions. Tier 1 ISPs form the global backbone.

discovery.text: Tier 1 providers peer with each other for free. Everyone else pays someone upstream for transit.

twist.text: Your ISP can see which domains you visit. That's why VPNs and encrypted DNS matter for privacy.

climax.text: In some countries, a single ISP controls nearly all access. That gives them enormous power over citizens.

punchline.text: Your ISP is your gateway. Choose wisely.

quiz.question: What is a Tier 1 ISP?
options: A provider that forms part of the internet's global backbone
An ISP that only serves residential customers
